endpoints.name: POST /exports/activity.json
endpoints.description: Begins an export of your activity history. The activity will be exported to a zip archive containing a single file named activity.csv in the same format as you would be able to export from your account's activity view. It includes the following fields: Date, Email Address, Sender, Subject, Status, Tags, Opens, Clicks, Bounce Detail. If you have configured any custom metadata fields, they will be included in the exported data.

endpoints.name: POST /exports/info.json
endpoints.description: Returns information about an export job. If the export job's state is 'complete', the returned data will include a URL you can use to fetch the results. Every export job produces a zip archive, but the format of the archive is distinct for each job type. The api calls that initiate exports include more details about the output format for that job type.

endpoints.name: POST /exports/list.json
endpoints.description: Returns a list of your exports.

endpoints.name: POST /exports/rejects.json
endpoints.description: Begins an export of your rejection blacklist. The blacklist will be exported to a zip archive containing a single file named rejects.csv that includes the following fields: email, reason, detail, created_at, expires_at, last_event_at, expires_at.

endpoints.name: POST /exports/whitelist.json
endpoints.description: Begins an export of your rejection whitelist. The whitelist will be exported to a zip archive containing a single file named whitelist.csv that includes the following fields: email, detail, created_at.

endpoints.name: POST /inbound/add-domain.json
endpoints.description: Add an inbound domain to your account

endpoints.name: POST /inbound/add-route.json
endpoints.description: Add a new mailbox route to an inbound domain

endpoints.name: POST /inbound/check-domain.json
endpoints.description: Check the MX settings for an inbound domain. The domain must have already been added with the add-domain call

endpoints.name: POST /inbound/delete-domain.json
endpoints.description: Delete an inbound domain from the account. All mail will stop routing for this domain immediately.

endpoints.name: POST /inbound/delete-route.json
endpoints.description: Delete an existing inbound mailbox route

endpoints.name: POST /inbound/domains.json
endpoints.description: List the domains that have been configured for inbound delivery

endpoints.name: POST /inbound/routes.json
endpoints.description: List the mailbox routes defined for an inbound domain

endpoints.name: POST /inbound/send-raw.json
endpoints.description: Take a raw MIME document destined for a domain with inbound domains set up, and send it to the inbound hook exactly as if it had been sent over SMTP

endpoints.name: POST /inbound/update-route.json
endpoints.description: Update the pattern or webhook of an existing inbound mailbox route. If null is provided for any fields, the values will remain unchanged.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/cancel-warmup.json
endpoints.description: Cancels the warmup process for a dedicated IP.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/check-custom-dns.json
endpoints.description: Tests whether a domain name is valid for use as the custom reverse DNS for a dedicated IP.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/create-pool.json
endpoints.description: Creates a pool and returns it. If a pool already exists with this name, no action will be performed.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/delete-pool.json
endpoints.description: Deletes a pool. A pool must be empty before you can delete it, and you cannot delete your default pool.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/delete.json
endpoints.description: Deletes a dedicated IP. This is permanent and cannot be undone.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/info.json
endpoints.description: Retrieves information about a single dedicated ip.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/list-pools.json
endpoints.description: Lists your dedicated IP pools.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/list.json
endpoints.description: Lists your dedicated IPs.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/pool-info.json
endpoints.description: Describes a single dedicated IP pool.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/provision.json
endpoints.description: Requests an additional dedicated IP for your account. Accounts may have one outstanding request at any time, and provisioning requests are processed within 24 hours.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/set-custom-dns.json
endpoints.description: Configures the custom DNS name for a dedicated IP.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/set-pool.json
endpoints.description: Moves a dedicated IP to a different pool.

endpoints.name: POST /ips/start-warmup.json
endpoints.description: Begins the warmup process for a dedicated IP. During the warmup process, Mandrill will gradually increase the percentage of your mail that is sent over the warming-up IP, over a period of roughly 30 days. The rest of your mail will be sent over shared IPs or other dedicated IPs in the same pool.

endpoints.name: POST /messages/cancel-scheduled.json
endpoints.description: Cancels a scheduled email.

endpoints.name: POST /messages/content.json
endpoints.description: Get the full content of a recently sent message

endpoints.name: POST /messages/info.json
endpoints.description: Get the information for a single recently sent message

endpoints.name: POST /messages/list-scheduled.json
endpoints.description: Queries your scheduled emails by sender or recipient, or both.

endpoints.name: POST /messages/parse.json
endpoints.description: Parse the full MIME document for an email message, returning the content of the message broken into its constituent pieces

endpoints.name: POST /messages/reschedule.json
endpoints.description: Reschedules a scheduled email.

endpoints.name: POST /messages/search-time-series.json
endpoints.description: Search the content of recently sent messages and return the aggregated hourly stats for matching messages

endpoints.name: POST /messages/search.json
endpoints.description: Search the content of recently sent messages and optionally narrow by date range, tags and senders. This method may be called up to 20 times per minute. If you need the data more often, you can use /messages/info.json to get the information for a single message, or webhooks to push activity to your own application for querying.

endpoints.name: POST /messages/send-raw.json
endpoints.description: Take a raw MIME document for a message, and send it exactly as if it were sent through Mandrill's SMTP servers

endpoints.name: POST /messages/send-template.json
endpoints.description: Send a new transactional message through Mandrill using a template

endpoints.name: POST /messages/send.json
endpoints.description: Send a new transactional message through Mandrill

endpoints.name: POST /metadata/add.json
endpoints.description: Add a new custom metadata field to be indexed for the account.

endpoints.name: POST /metadata/delete.json
endpoints.description: Delete an existing custom metadata field. Deletion isn't instataneous, and /metadata/list will continue to return the field until the asynchronous deletion process is complete.

endpoints.name: POST /metadata/list.json
endpoints.description: Get the list of custom metadata fields indexed for the account.

endpoints.name: POST /metadata/update.json
endpoints.description: Update an existing custom metadata field.

endpoints.name: POST /rejects/add.json
endpoints.description: Adds an email to your email rejection blacklist. Addresses that you add manually will never expire and there is no reputation penalty for removing them from your blacklist. Attempting to blacklist an address that has been whitelisted will have no effect.

endpoints.name: POST /rejects/delete.json
endpoints.description: Deletes an email rejection. There is no limit to how many rejections you can remove from your blacklist, but keep in mind that each deletion has an affect on your reputation.

endpoints.name: POST /rejects/list.json
endpoints.description: Retrieves your email rejection blacklist. You can provide an email address to limit the results. Returns up to 1000 results. By default, entries that have expired are excluded from the results; set include_expired to true to include them.

endpoints.name: POST /senders/add-domain.json
endpoints.description: Adds a sender domain to your account. Sender domains are added automatically as you send, but you can use this call to add them ahead of time.

endpoints.name: POST /senders/check-domain.json
endpoints.description: Checks the SPF and DKIM settings for a domain. If you haven't already added this domain to your account, it will be added automatically.

endpoints.name: POST /senders/domains.json
endpoints.description: Returns the sender domains that have been added to this account.

endpoints.name: POST /senders/info.json
endpoints.description: Return more detailed information about a single sender, including aggregates of recent stats

endpoints.name: POST /senders/list.json
endpoints.description: Return the senders that have tried to use this account.

endpoints.name: POST /senders/time-series.json
endpoints.description: Return the recent history (hourly stats for the last 30 days) for a sender

endpoints.name: POST /senders/verify-domain.json
endpoints.description: Sends a verification email in order to verify ownership of a domain. Domain verification is an optional step to confirm ownership of a domain. Once a domain has been verified in a Mandrill account, other accounts may not have their messages signed by that domain unless they also verify the domain. This prevents other Mandrill accounts from sending mail signed by your domain.

endpoints.name: POST /subaccounts/add.json
endpoints.description: Add a new subaccount

endpoints.name: POST /subaccounts/delete.json
endpoints.description: Delete an existing subaccount. Any email related to the subaccount will be saved, but stats will be removed and any future sending calls to this subaccount will fail.

endpoints.name: POST /subaccounts/info.json
endpoints.description: Given the ID of an existing subaccount, return the data about it

endpoints.name: POST /subaccounts/list.json
endpoints.description: Get the list of subaccounts defined for the account, optionally filtered by a prefix

endpoints.name: POST /subaccounts/pause.json
endpoints.description: Pause a subaccount's sending. Any future emails delivered to this subaccount will be queued for a maximum of 3 days until the subaccount is resumed.

endpoints.name: POST /subaccounts/resume.json
endpoints.description: Resume a paused subaccount's sending

endpoints.name: POST /subaccounts/update.json
endpoints.description: Update an existing subaccount

endpoints.name: POST /tags/all-time-series.json
endpoints.description: Return the recent history (hourly stats for the last 30 days) for all tags

endpoints.name: POST /tags/delete.json
endpoints.description: Deletes a tag permanently. Deleting a tag removes the tag from any messages that have been sent, and also deletes the tag's stats. There is no way to undo this operation, so use it carefully.

endpoints.name: POST /tags/info.json
endpoints.description: Return more detailed information about a single tag, including aggregates of recent stats

endpoints.name: POST /tags/list.json
endpoints.description: Return all of the user-defined tag information

endpoints.name: POST /tags/time-series.json
endpoints.description: Return the recent history (hourly stats for the last 30 days) for a tag

endpoints.name: POST /templates/add.json
endpoints.description: Add a new template

endpoints.name: POST /templates/delete.json
endpoints.description: Delete a template

endpoints.name: POST /templates/info.json
endpoints.description: Get the information for an existing template

endpoints.name: POST /templates/list.json
endpoints.description: Return a list of all the templates available to this user

endpoints.name: POST /templates/publish.json
endpoints.description: Publish the content for the template. Any new messages sent using this template will start using the content that was previously in draft.

endpoints.name: POST /templates/render.json
endpoints.description: Inject content and optionally merge fields into a template, returning the HTML that results

endpoints.name: POST /templates/time-series.json
endpoints.description: Return the recent history (hourly stats for the last 30 days) for a template

endpoints.name: POST /templates/update.json
endpoints.description: Update the code for an existing template. If null is provided for any fields, the values will remain unchanged.

endpoints.name: POST /urls/add-tracking-domain.json
endpoints.description: Add a tracking domain to your account

endpoints.name: POST /urls/check-tracking-domain.json
endpoints.description: Checks the CNAME settings for a tracking domain. The domain must have been added already with the add-tracking-domain call

endpoints.name: POST /urls/list.json
endpoints.description: Get the 100 most clicked URLs

endpoints.name: POST /urls/search.json
endpoints.description: Return the 100 most clicked URLs that match the search query given

endpoints.name: POST /urls/time-series.json
endpoints.description: Return the recent history (hourly stats for the last 30 days) for a url

endpoints.name: POST /urls/tracking-domains.json
endpoints.description: Get the list of tracking domains set up for this account

endpoints.name: POST /users/info.json
endpoints.description: Return the information about the API-connected user

endpoints.name: POST /users/ping.json
endpoints.description: Validate an API key and respond to a ping

endpoints.name: POST /users/ping2.json
endpoints.description: Validate an API key and respond to a ping (anal JSON parser version)

endpoints.name: POST /users/senders.json
endpoints.description: Return the senders that have tried to use this account, both verified and unverified

endpoints.name: POST /webhooks/add.json
endpoints.description: Add a new webhook

endpoints.name: POST /webhooks/delete.json
endpoints.description: Delete an existing webhook

endpoints.name: POST /webhooks/info.json
endpoints.description: Given the ID of an existing webhook, return the data about it

endpoints.name: POST /webhooks/list.json
endpoints.description: Get the list of all webhooks defined on the account

endpoints.name: POST /webhooks/update.json
endpoints.description: Update an existing webhook

endpoints.name: POST /whitelists/add.json
endpoints.description: Adds an email to your email rejection whitelist. If the address is currently on your blacklist, that blacklist entry will be removed automatically.

endpoints.name: POST /whitelists/delete.json
endpoints.description: Removes an email address from the whitelist.

endpoints.name: POST /whitelists/list.json
endpoints.description: Retrieves your email rejection whitelist. You can provide an email address or search prefix to limit the results. Returns up to 1000 results.
